perturbation.original_text: The Publishers shall have the right to renew the term of the Services by notice in writing to HealthGate to be given on or before 30 September 1999. If the Publishers exercise their right to renew, the term of the Services shall be extended by one further year, up to and including 28 February 2001. The Use Fees shall remain the same as in the Initial Period and the fee for the Services shall not exceed $7000 for additional journals, $2000 maintenance fee on existing journals and $2000 per Gigabyte.
perturbation.changed_text: The Publishers shall have the right to renew the term of the Services by notice in writing to HealthGate to be given on or before 30 September 1999. If the Publishers exercise their right to renew, the term of the Services shall be extended by one further year, up to and including 28 February 2001. The Use Fees shall be subject to change at HealthGate's discretion and the fee for the Services shall not exceed $7000 for additional journals, $2000 maintenance fee on existing journals and $2000 per Gigabyte.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 17.1, the original text clearly states that 'The Use Fees shall remain the same as in the Initial Period'. However, the modified version introduces uncertainty by stating 'The Use Fees shall be subject to change at HealthGate's discretion'. This directly contradicts the initial statement guaranteeing fixed Use Fees, creating an in-text contradiction. This change impacts the financial predictability for the Publishers and is contradictory to what was stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Publishers shall have the right to renew the term of the Services by notice in writing to HealthGate to be given on or before 30 September 1999. If the Publishers exercise their right to renew, the term of the Services shall be extended by one further year, up to and including 28 February 2001. The Use Fees shall be subject to change at HealthGate's discretion and the fee for the Services shall not exceed $7000 for additional journals, $2000 maintenance fee on existing journals and $2000 per Gigabyte.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "The Use Fees shall remain the same as in the Initial Period," establishing a fixed fee structure. The changed text introduces the clause "The Use Fees shall be subject to change at HealthGate's discretion," directly contradicting the earlier statement. This alteration creates a clear in-text contradiction as it provides conflicting terms regarding how fees are determined, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about pricing.

perturbation.original_text: HealthGate shall replace and upgrade the Software to satisfy the requirements of the Specification at no extra cost to the Publishers.
perturbation.changed_text: HealthGate shall replace and upgrade the Software to satisfy the requirements of the Specification, however additional costs may be incurred by the Publishers for these upgrades.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 15, the original text explicitly states that the software upgrades to meet specification requirements will be at 'no extra cost to the Publishers'. The modified text contradicts this by saying, 'however additional costs may be incurred by the Publishers for these upgrades'. This contradictory statement creates uncertainty regarding the financial obligations of the Publishers for upgrades, which clashes with the assurance of 'no extra cost' in the original statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HealthGate shall replace and upgrade the Software to satisfy the requirements of the Specification, however additional costs may be incurred by the Publishers for these upgrades.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that software upgrades will be at no extra cost. The changed text introduces the possibility of additional costs for these upgrades. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Since the changed text directly contradicts the original agreement regarding costs, it creates a clear in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', where a clause is placed with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations of each party.

perturbation.original_text: HealthGate will host the Site in accordance with the Specification for the period for the Services in Clause 3.
perturbation.changed_text: HealthGate will make an effort to host the Site in accordance with the Specification for the period for the Services in Clause 3.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 11, the original text states that 'HealthGate will host the Site in accordance with the Specification'. However, the modified version introduces uncertainty by stating 'HealthGate will make an effort to host the Site in accordance with the Specification'. This directly contradicts the initial statement guaranteeing fixed Use Fees, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HealthGate will make an effort to host the Site in accordance with the Specification for the period for the Services in Clause 3.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a firm commitment: "HealthGate will host the Site in accordance with the Specification." The changed text weakens this commitment to: "HealthGate will make an effort to host the Site in accordance with the Specification." This change introduces a degree of uncertainty regarding HealthGate's hosting obligation, directly contradicting any clause, such as a service level agreement, that relies on a guaranteed level of service as initially defined. Since the contradicted text states the same clause with the changed text, it creates a contradiction.
